message:
feat(python): sliding_window_view and as_strided wrappers

Exposes the two numpy.lib.stride_tricks primitives via the fnp-python
PyO3 surface as direct passthroughs:

- sliding_window_view(x, window_shape, axis=None, *, subok=False,
  writeable=False): returns a strided view with a leading window axis
  per element of window_shape; axis restricts which axes are rolled
  (None rolls every axis). Default output is read-only, matching numpy.
- as_strided(x, shape=None, strides=None, subok=False, writeable=True):
  the low-level primitive that sliding_window_view is built on; shape
  and byte-level strides are taken verbatim with no bounds checking.

Live-NumPy parity tests cover sliding_window_view across 1-D scalar
window, 1-D tuple window, 2-D all-axis windowing, 2-D axis-restricted
windowing, the oversized-window ValueError, and the writeable=False
default; as_strided is tested against a same-itemsize shrinking view
and an overlapping sliding-window-style view.

Avoids collision with panes 2/4 currently in the numpy.ma and
numpy.testing wrapper domains; this is the numpy.lib.stride_tricks
domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
